---
title: "Device Selector (GPU/CPU)"
---

## Overview

The Deep Learning Properties Widget includes an optional device selector that allows users
to switch between GPU (CUDA) and CPU inference at runtime. This group box only appears when
CUDA is available at runtime (i.e., CUDA build + GPU detected). On CPU-only builds or
systems without a GPU, the selector is hidden and inference runs on CPU automatically.

## Architecture

The `DeviceManager` singleton (`src/DeepLearning/device/DeviceManager.hpp`) owns the active
`torch::Device`. Because the properties widget must not include `<torch/torch.h>` (to avoid
the Qt `slots` macro conflict), three static proxy methods on `SlotAssembler` bridge the gap:

| SlotAssembler static method | DeviceManager method called |
|-----------------------------|-----------------------------|
| `isCudaAvailable()`        | `DeviceManager::cudaAvailable()` |
| `currentDeviceName()`      | `DeviceManager::instance().device()` (returns human-readable string) |
| `setDeviceByName(name)`    | `DeviceManager::instance().setDevice(...)` |

## UI Behaviour

- **Default**: GPU is selected when CUDA is available.
- **Hidden**: The "Device" group box is not created at all on CPU-only systems.
- **Effect**: Changing the combo immediately updates `DeviceManager`. All subsequent
  inference calls (single frame, batch, recurrent) use the new device.
